title: Newsletter MVP (email-only with WhatsApp channel CTA)

tasks.title: Verify chore/vps-migration merged and audit newsletter prerequisites
acceptanceCriteria: git log confirms chore/vps-migration is on main
packages/notifications/src/transports/email/resend-transport.ts exports BrevoEmailTransport
apps/api/src/cron/bootstrap.ts and schedules.manifest.ts exist
apps/api/src/utils/redis.ts exports Redis client
RetryService instantiated with redis=null documented as known gap to fix in T-101-46
tasks.description: Read-only verification task. Confirm the three runtime primitives from chore/vps-migration are present: Brevo email transport, node-cron infra, Redis client. Document findings. No code changes. If any primitive is missing, block Phase 1 and notify. No test required — this is a preflight check.

findings.brevoTransport: OK — packages/notifications/src/transports/email/resend-transport.ts:39 exports class BrevoEmailTransport. Alias `ResendEmailTransport = BrevoEmailTransport` retained at :138 for backward-compat. Uses native fetch (no SDK) to avoid ESM/CJS conflict.
findings.cronInfra: OK — apps/api/src/cron/bootstrap.ts and apps/api/src/cron/schedules.manifest.ts both exist. 19 jobs registered. HOSPEDA_CRON_ADAPTER=node-cron in prod, manual in dev/tests.
findings.redisClient: OK — apps/api/src/utils/redis.ts exports getRedisClient(), disconnectRedis(), resetRedisState(). Uses env.HOSPEDA_REDIS_URL with graceful undefined fallback. Connection lazy, retryStrategy capped at 5 attempts.
findings.retryService: RetryService instantiated in apps/api/src/cron/jobs/notification-schedule.job.ts:604 with redisClient ?? null (NOT hardcoded null). When Redis is configured, retry logic activates; when not, logs warning and skips. No gap to fix in T-101-46 — runtime config dependency only.

missingDeps.tiptap: NOT installed in apps/admin/package.json. @tiptap/react and @tiptap/starter-kit must be added before T-101-29. Plan: prepend `pnpm --filter=admin add @tiptap/react @tiptap/starter-kit` step to T-101-29.

findings.sqlNumbering: tech-analysis assumed manual SQL numbers 0027/0028/0029 but the last in main is 0021. Numbers 0022..0026 are free. Decision: use 0022 (subscriber unique active), 0023 (delivery constraints), 0024 (seed legacy opt-ins) for continuity. Affects T-101-04 (path 0022), T-101-06 (paths 0022+0023), T-101-03 (path 0024).
findings.phase1Decision: GO. No primitives missing. Phase 1 can start.

tasks.title: Fix buggy /me/newsletter/toggle endpoint (write settings.newsletter not notifications.allowEmails)
acceptanceCriteria: PATCH /api/v1/protected/user/newsletter writes to user.settings.newsletter (not settings.notifications.allowEmails)
Endpoint is annotated with @deprecated JSDoc noting V2 removal
Regression test: POST with { newsletter: true } reads back settings.newsletter=true
Regression test: previous wrong field settings.notifications.allowEmails is NOT modified
pnpm typecheck passes in apps/api
tasks.description: Fix the existing buggy endpoint at apps/api/src/routes/user/protected/newsletter.ts. The current code writes to the wrong JSONB path (notifications.allowEmails instead of newsletter). Fix the JSONB path update to target settings.newsletter. Add @deprecated JSDoc. Write a regression test that verifies the correct field is written and the wrong field is untouched. This is a bug fix per AC-101-14.1.

tasks.title: Create manual SQL for legacy opt-in user migration (0029_newsletter_seed_existing_optins.sql)
acceptanceCriteria: SQL file uses ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING (idempotent)
Inserts users WHERE settings->>'newsletter'='true' AND deleted_at IS NULL
Sets channel='email', status='active', source='migration'
Derives locale from settings->>'languageWeb' falling back to 'es'
SQL file added to apply-postgres-extras.sh execution order after 0028
Integration test confirms idempotency: running twice produces no duplicates
tasks.description: Create packages/db/src/migrations/manual/0029_newsletter_seed_existing_optins.sql with the INSERT ... SELECT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING pattern from the tech-analysis §2.2. Add the script to the apply-postgres-extras.sh execution list after 0028. Write an integration test in packages/db/test/integration/newsletter.test.ts confirming idempotency. Implements AC-101-14.2 and AC-101-14.3. Depends on T-101-07 (tables must exist first).

tasks.title: Create Drizzle table schema for newsletter_subscribers
acceptanceCriteria: All enum types declared (channel, status, source, locale)
Table columns match tech-analysis §2.1 exactly (id, userId, email, channel, status, locale, source, consent fields, all timestamps)
Drizzle-managed indexes declared (status, locale, channel, email BTREEs)
File exported from packages/db/src/schemas/index.ts
pnpm typecheck passes in packages/db
tasks.description: Create packages/db/src/schemas/newsletter/newsletter-subscriber.table.ts following the exact schema from tech-analysis §2.1. Declare the four pgEnum types. Add Drizzle-managed indexes. Export from the newsletter index.ts and the main schemas index.ts. No manual SQL here — that is handled by T-101-06 and T-101-03.

qualityGate.tests: n/a — schema-only, covered by T-101-07 integration test

tasks.title: Create Drizzle table schema for newsletter_campaigns
acceptanceCriteria: newsletterCampaignStatusEnum and newsletterCampaignLocaleFilterEnum declared
All columns present: id, title, subject, bodyJson (jsonb), status, localeFilter, totalRecipients, totalSoftcapped, sentAt, scheduledFor, createdBy, audit timestamps
Status and createdBy indexes declared
Exported from newsletter schema index
pnpm typecheck passes in packages/db
tasks.description: Create packages/db/src/schemas/newsletter/newsletter-campaign.table.ts per tech-analysis §2.1. Include both enums. Note scheduledFor column exists but is unused in MVP (reserved for V2). Export from newsletter index.

qualityGate.tests: n/a — covered by integration test in T-101-07

tasks.title: Create Drizzle table schema for newsletter_campaign_deliveries + manual SQL constraints
acceptanceCriteria: Delivery table has all columns from tech-analysis §2.1 (no deletedAt — immutable)
newsletterDeliveryStatusEnum declared
Manual SQL 0027 creates partial unique index on newsletter_subscribers(user_id, channel) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL
Manual SQL 0028 creates unique index on newsletter_campaign_deliveries(campaign_id, subscriber_id, channel) and partial pending index
Both SQL files added to apply-postgres-extras.sh in order before 0029
pnpm typecheck passes in packages/db
tasks.description: Create newsletter-campaign-delivery.table.ts per tech-analysis §2.1. Create manual SQL files 0027 and 0028. Register them in apply-postgres-extras.sh. The delivery table has no deletedAt — records are immutable, cancelled via status='skipped'. Partial unique indexes cannot be expressed in Drizzle and must go in manual SQL.

tasks.title: Run db push and write DB integration tests for all three newsletter tables
acceptanceCriteria: drizzle-kit push creates all three tables without error
apply-postgres-extras.sh applies 0027, 0028, 0029 without error
Integration test: UNIQUE (user_id, channel) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL is enforced
Integration test: UNIQUE (campaign_id, subscriber_id, channel) on deliveries is enforced
Integration test: ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING on migration seed insert
pnpm test passes for packages/db
tasks.description: After all three table schemas exist, run pnpm db:push to create the tables. Then run apply-postgres-extras.sh to apply manual SQL constraints. Write packages/db/test/integration/newsletter.test.ts testing the three unique constraints and migration idempotency. This validates the full DB layer before any service code is written.

tasks.title: Create Zod schemas for all three newsletter entities
acceptanceCriteria: All entity schemas match Drizzle table definitions (no type drift)
TiptapDocumentSchema exported from campaign schema
AdminSearchSchema for subscribers extends AdminSearchBaseSchema with status/locale/source/channel filters
AdminSearchSchema for campaigns extends AdminSearchBaseSchema with status filter
HTTP schemas define request/response shapes for all endpoints
All schemas exported from packages/schemas index
pnpm typecheck passes in packages/schemas
tasks.description: Create all 12 schema files per tech-analysis §5. Key schemas: NewsletterSubscriberSchema, NewsletterCampaignSchema (with TiptapDocumentSchema), NewsletterDeliverySchema. CRUD schemas for create/update operations. Admin search schemas with filter fields. HTTP schemas for request/response shapes. Export all from the entities index. These are the single source of truth consumed by services and routes.

qualityGate.tests: unit tests for schema validation edge cases (empty body, max length violations)

tasks.title: Add NEWSLETTER_MANAGE permissions to PermissionEnum and seed to admin roles
acceptanceCriteria: PermissionCategoryEnum gains NEWSLETTER_CAMPAIGN and NEWSLETTER_SUBSCRIBER categories
PermissionEnum gains: NEWSLETTER_CAMPAIGN_VIEW, NEWSLETTER_CAMPAIGN_WRITE, NEWSLETTER_CAMPAIGN_SEND, NEWSLETTER_SUBSCRIBER_VIEW
Seed data assigns all four permissions to super_admin and admin roles
pnpm db:seed runs without error
pnpm typecheck passes in packages/schemas and packages/seed
tasks.description: Add the four new PermissionEnum values and two PermissionCategoryEnum values per tech-analysis §5.3. Update seed data to assign all four permissions to super_admin and admin roles. Verify pnpm db:seed completes without FK errors. Services will import and check these permissions.

qualityGate.tests: n/a — seed is validated by running it

tasks.title: Implement HMAC token helpers (verification token + unsubscribe token)
acceptanceCriteria: generateVerificationToken({ subscriberId, channel, subscribedAt, secret }) returns base64url string
verifyVerificationToken({ token, secret, ttlHours }) returns { subscriberId, channel } or throws TokenExpiredError / InvalidTokenError
generateUnsubscribeToken({ subscriberId, channel, secret }) returns stable deterministic base64url string
verifyUnsubscribeToken({ token, secret }) returns { subscriberId, channel } or throws InvalidTokenError
HOSPEDA_NEWSLETTER_HMAC_SECRET_PREV fallback rotation supported
Unit tests cover: valid token, expired token (> 72h), tampered token, stable unsubscribe token determinism
All functions are pure (no DB, no side effects) — 90%+ line coverage
tasks.description: Create newsletter-token.helpers.ts with pure HMAC-SHA256 token generation and verification per tech-analysis §9.1. Verification token encodes subscribedAt in payload for TTL without DB query. Unsubscribe token is deterministic. Support HMAC_SECRET_PREV fallback for key rotation. Write unit tests as a companion file. These helpers are consumed by NewsletterSubscriberService.

tasks.title: Add NotificationCategory.NEWSLETTER and newsletter notification types to @repo/notifications
acceptanceCriteria: NotificationType gains NEWSLETTER_VERIFICATION, NEWSLETTER_WELCOME, NEWSLETTER_CAMPAIGN
NotificationCategory gains NEWSLETTER
NOTIFICATION_CATEGORY_MAP maps NEWSLETTER_VERIFICATION to TRANSACTIONAL, NEWSLETTER_WELCOME and NEWSLETTER_CAMPAIGN to NEWSLETTER
pnpm typecheck passes in packages/notifications
tasks.description: Add the three new NotificationType values and the NEWSLETTER category per tech-analysis §6.2. Update NOTIFICATION_CATEGORY_MAP. This is a prerequisite for creating the email templates in T-101-13.

tasks.title: Implement NewsletterSubscriberService
acceptanceCriteria: All methods from tech-analysis §4.1 implemented: subscribe, verifyToken, resendVerification, unsubscribeByToken, unsubscribeAuthenticated, getStatus, getEligibleForCampaign, adminList, getStats
subscribe() handles all 4 subscriber state transitions correctly (active/pending/unsubscribed/new)
verifyToken() marks status=active, verified_at=now() on success
getEligibleForCampaign() implements soft-cap rolling window query
Result<T> return type throughout
Permission checks use PermissionEnum (never role checks)
Unit tests cover all state transitions in subscribe() and soft-cap exclusion logic
90%+ line coverage on service file
tasks.description: Implement NewsletterSubscriberService extending BaseCrudService. Key complexity is in subscribe() (4 state machine transitions) and getEligibleForCampaign() (soft-cap SQL query per tech-analysis §8.4). All email sending is delegated to @repo/notifications. Create companion permissions file. Write unit tests mocking the DB and notification service. This is the most complex service in the spec.

tasks.title: Create newsletter email templates (verification, welcome, campaign wrapper)
acceptanceCriteria: newsletter-verify-email.tsx renders: greeting with firstName, CTA button with token URL, plain-text fallback link, ignore note
newsletter-welcome-email.tsx renders: welcome message, optional WhatsApp CTA when waChannelUrl is set
newsletter-campaign.tsx wraps pre-rendered bodyHtml with header and unsubscribe footer
Test email prefix '[PRUEBA] ' and test banner injected when isTest=true
Unsubscribe link in footer uses stable HMAC token passed as prop
i18n keys used from @repo/i18n (es primary), not hardcoded strings
No open pixel injection — Brevo handles tracking natively
tasks.description: Create three email templates per tech-analysis §6.2. The campaign wrapper receives pre-rendered bodyHtml and adds header and footer. Verification template encodes token URL. Welcome template optionally shows WhatsApp CTA. All templates use @repo/i18n for strings. Test mode is toggled by isTest prop. Templates are consumed by NewsletterSubscriberService (verify/welcome) and NewsletterDeliveryService (campaign).

qualityGate.tests: snapshot tests for each template render

tasks.title: Implement NewsletterCampaignService
acceptanceCriteria: All methods from tech-analysis §4.2 implemented: create, update, softDelete, send, testSend, cancel, computeMetrics, getFailedDeliveries, closeSentCampaigns
send() validates status='draft' before proceeding
send() wraps bulk INSERT deliveries + campaign status UPDATE in withServiceTransaction()
send() calls NewsletterDeliveryService.enqueueBatches() AFTER transaction commits
cancel() sets campaign to cancelled and calls bulkSkipPending()
closeSentCampaigns() uses EXISTS subquery from tech-analysis §8.5
Unit tests cover send() happy path, campaign_not_sendable error, zero eligible subscribers case, cancel flow
90%+ line coverage
tasks.description: Implement NewsletterCampaignService extending BaseCrudService. The critical method is send() — its 7-step flow (per tech-analysis §4.2) must be transactional through step 6, with BullMQ enqueue in step 5 happening after transaction commits. closeSentCampaigns() is called by the cron job in T-101-48. Write unit tests mocking NewsletterSubscriberService, NewsletterDeliveryService, and the DB.

tasks.title: Move tiptap-renderer to @repo/utils and create email-safe HTML transformer in @repo/notifications
acceptanceCriteria: packages/utils/src/tiptap-renderer.ts exports renderTiptapContent() — moved from apps/web/src/lib/
horizontalRule node type handled (renders <hr style='...' />)
apps/web import path updated to import from @repo/utils
packages/notifications/src/utils/tiptap-email-renderer.ts exports renderTiptapEmailContent() that calls base renderer then applies inline styles

Email transformer adds inline styles to ,

,

-

, strips disallowed tags
pnpm typecheck passes in packages/utils, packages/notifications, apps/web
tasks.description: Extract renderTiptapContent() from apps/web/src/lib/tiptap-renderer.ts to packages/utils/src/tiptap-renderer.ts per tech-analysis §7. Fix the missing horizontalRule case. Create tiptap-email-renderer.ts in @repo/notifications that wraps the base renderer with email-safe inline style injection. Update apps/web import. Both apps/admin and apps/api (via service-core) will import from their respective packages.

qualityGate.tests: unit tests for email transformer (inline style injection, tag stripping)

tasks.title: Implement NewsletterDeliveryService + Brevo batch send helper
acceptanceCriteria: brevo-batch.ts exports sendBatch() using native fetch to POST /v3/smtp/email with messageVersions array
sendBatch() returns SendBatchResult with messageIds per recipient
enqueueBatches() groups delivery IDs into batches of HOSPEDA_NEWSLETTER_BATCH_SIZE (default 100) and calls BullMQ addBulk
processBatch() implements 8-step flow from tech-analysis §4.3 (status checks, subscriber check, render, batch build, Brevo call, mapping, bulk update, error retry)
processBatch() skips non-pending and non-active-subscriber deliveries (idempotency)
bulkSkipPending() bulk-updates pending deliveries to skipped for a campaign
Unit tests cover processBatch() happy path, skip logic, Brevo error leading to job retry
90%+ line coverage
tasks.description: Create packages/notifications/src/transports/email/brevo-batch.ts — the Brevo multi-recipient batch helper (no SDK, native fetch). Implement NewsletterDeliveryService with all methods from tech-analysis §4.3. processBatch() is the critical path: check status, render email HTML via tiptap-email-renderer, build Brevo batch, call sendBatch(), bulk-update. enqueueBatches() creates BullMQ jobs. Companion test file required.

tasks.title: Implement NewsletterTrackingService (Brevo webhook event processor)
acceptanceCriteria: processBrevoWebhookEvent() maps all 8 Brevo event types correctly (delivered, hard_bounce, soft_bounce, spam/complained, unsubscribed, invalid_email, opened, click)
opened and click events use UPDATE WHERE opened_at IS NULL / first_click_at IS NULL (first event only)
Lookup strategy: find delivery by provider_message_id, fallback to subscriber by email
All newsletter services exported from packages/service-core index
Unit tests cover each event type mapping
90%+ line coverage
tasks.description: Implement NewsletterTrackingService per tech-analysis §4.4. The processBrevoWebhookEvent() method handles all Brevo webhook events. Critically: open/click only record the FIRST event (idempotent). Hard bounces change subscriber status to bounced. Spam complaints change subscriber to complained. Export all newsletter services from service-core index.

qualityGate.tests: unit tests for all event types, 90% coverage

tasks.title: Register env vars for newsletter system in @repo/config
acceptanceCriteria: HOSPEDA_NEWSLETTER_HMAC_SECRET registered (required, secret, min 32 chars)
HOSPEDA_NEWSLETTER_HMAC_SECRET_PREV registered (optional, secret, rotation support)
HOSPEDA_BREVO_WEBHOOK_SECRET registered (required, secret)
HOSPEDA_NEWSLETTER_SOFTCAP_DAYS registered (optional, number, default 7)
HOSPEDA_NEWSLETTER_BATCH_SIZE registered (optional, number, default 100)
HOSPEDA_NEWSLETTER_WORKER_CONCURRENCY registered (optional, number, default 5)
PUBLIC_HOSPEDA_WHATSAPP_CHANNEL_URL registered (optional, public, web-side)
All vars added to apps/api/.env.example with placeholder values
Zod validation added in apps/api/src/utils/env.ts
pnpm env:check passes
tasks.description: Register all 7 new env vars per tech-analysis §15. Follow the existing pattern in env-registry.hospeda.ts with full metadata (description, type, required, secret, defaultValue, exampleValue, apps, category). Add Zod validation to apps/api/src/utils/env.ts. Update .env.example files. After implementation, run pnpm env:sync for each environment.

tasks.title: Add i18n keys for subscription flow surfaces (footer, popover, verify pages, unsubscribe, account, email templates, WhatsApp CTA)
acceptanceCriteria: All i18n keys from tech-analysis §13 added to es/ (primary source)
Same strings copied verbatim to en/ and pt/ as fallback (per NFR 6.5)
Keys cover: footer.newsletter.*, newsletter.authPopover.*, newsletter.verify.*, newsletter.error.*, newsletter.unsubscribe.*, account.newsletter.*, newsletter.whatsapp.*, email.newsletter.*
No hardcoded Spanish strings in any UI component for these surfaces
pnpm typecheck passes in packages/i18n
tasks.description: Add all user-facing i18n keys from tech-analysis §13 to all three locale files simultaneously. es/ is the primary locale; en/ and pt/ get the same Spanish strings as fallback per NFR 6.5. Create newsletter.json namespace file if not already present. All UI components in subsequent tasks will use t() with these keys — never hardcode strings.

qualityGate.tests: n/a — key presence verified by TypeScript types

tasks.title: Implement protected API: POST /newsletter/subscribe and GET /newsletter/status
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/v1/protected/newsletter/subscribe: resolves email from session, locale from header, calls NewsletterSubscriberService.subscribe()
Returns 200 with { status: 'pending_verification' | 'active' | 'already_pending' }
Rate limit: 5 req/min per user applied
GET /api/v1/protected/newsletter/status: returns subscription status for the authenticated user
Integration tests cover: new subscriber, already active (returns active), already pending (returns pending_verification)
80%+ route coverage
tasks.description: Create apps/api/src/routes/newsletter/protected/index.ts with subscribe and status endpoints. Use createSimpleRoute or existing route factory patterns. Extract email from session, locale from request header. Apply rate limiting per NFR 6.2. Register routes in apps/api/src/routes/newsletter/index.ts. Write integration tests. These are the first routes consumed by the footer island.

tasks.title: Implement protected API: POST /newsletter/resend-verification and DELETE /newsletter/unsubscribe
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/v1/protected/newsletter/resend-verification: sends new verification email, returns 200 { sent: true } or 400 { error: 'already_active' | 'not_subscribed' }
DELETE /api/v1/protected/newsletter/unsubscribe: sets status=unsubscribed, returns 200 { status: 'unsubscribed' }
Rate limit on resend: 3 req/min per user
Integration tests cover: resend while pending (success), resend while active (400), unsubscribe while active (success), unsubscribe while not subscribed (400)
tasks.description: Extend the protected newsletter router with resend-verification and unsubscribe endpoints. resend-verification invalidates the old token (by updating subscribedAt) and sends a fresh verification email. Authenticated unsubscribe sets status=unsubscribed. Rate limit resend to 3/min. Add integration tests.

tasks.title: Implement public API: GET /newsletter/verify (token validation + redirect)
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/v1/public/newsletter/verify?token=... validates HMAC token
Valid token: updates status=active, verified_at=now(), redirects 302 to /{locale}/newsletter/confirmado/
Expired token: redirects to /{locale}/newsletter/error/?reason=token_expired
Invalid/tampered token: redirects to /{locale}/newsletter/error/?reason=invalid_token
Already-verified token: idempotent, redirects to confirmado/ without error
Rate limit: 10 req/min per IP
Integration tests cover all 4 response cases
tasks.description: Create public newsletter routes file. Implement GET verify endpoint — validate HMAC token via newsletter-token.helpers.ts, call NewsletterSubscriberService.verifyToken(), redirect based on result. Locale for redirect comes from token payload. Rate limit per IP. Write integration tests for all AC-101-04 scenarios.

qualityGate.tests: integration tests for all token states

tasks.title: Implement public API: GET /newsletter/unsubscribe (token-gated, no auth required)
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/v1/public/newsletter/unsubscribe?token=... validates stable HMAC unsubscribe token
Valid token: sets status=unsubscribed, unsubscribed_at=now(), redirects to /{locale}/newsletter/desuscripto/
Already unsubscribed: idempotent, still redirects to desuscripto/
Invalid token: redirects to /{locale}/newsletter/error/?reason=invalid_token
Rate limit: 20 req/min per IP
No login required at any point
Integration tests cover valid, idempotent, and invalid cases
tasks.description: Add GET /newsletter/unsubscribe to the public newsletter router. Uses stable unsubscribe token (no TTL). Calls NewsletterSubscriberService.unsubscribeByToken(). Implements AC-101-06.2 through AC-101-06.6. Rate limit 20/min per IP.

tasks.title: Create WhatsAppCTA component (env-gated, reusable)
acceptanceCriteria: Component renders nothing when PUBLIC_HOSPEDA_WHATSAPP_CHANNEL_URL is unset (AC-101-13.4)
Shows: WhatsApp icon, headline, description, CTA button opening URL in new tab
Fires client-side analytics event newsletter_wa_channel_clicked with source prop
Accessible: button has aria-label, not color-only indicator
Uses @repo/i18n keys for all strings (newsletter.whatsapp.*)
CSS uses --surface-warm CSS variable per UX §5.5
Tested via Vitest/RTL: renders null when env unset, renders correctly when set
tasks.description: Create a reusable WhatsAppCTA Astro component. The component accepts a source prop for analytics ('verification_success' or 'account_preferences'). Guard rendering on PUBLIC_HOSPEDA_WHATSAPP_CHANNEL_URL env var. Write unit test covering the null-render case and the render-with-URL case. This component is embedded in T-101-35 (verify page) and T-101-37 (account preferences).

qualityGate.tests: unit tests: null render + correct render

tasks.title: Implement NewsletterForm.client.tsx React island (footer subscribe form with 6 states)
acceptanceCriteria: Implements all 6 states: idle-guest, idle-auth, pending, pending-verification, already-active, error
Fetches /api/v1/protected/newsletter/status on mount (authenticated only) to determine initial state
Authenticated state: email pre-filled from session, read-only input
Guest state: editable empty input, lock icon badge, AuthRequiredPopover on click
AuthRequiredPopover: shows title/message/register CTA/login link per AC-101-02.3, closes on Escape/outside click
Loading spinner on submit, button disabled during flight
Inline error message on API failure, button re-enabled
aria-live='polite' region for status messages
Keyboard-navigable (Tab/Enter)
Hydration: client:visible
80%+ test coverage via Vitest + @testing-library/react
tasks.description: Create the NewsletterForm React island replacing the current inert static footer form. Implement all 6 visual states from UX §5.1. Create AuthRequiredPopover as a sub-component with focus trap and proper dismissal. All strings via @repo/i18n. Vanilla CSS (no Tailwind per web app convention). Test the state machine transitions and accessibility attributes.

qualityGate.tests: component tests for state transitions and accessibility, 80% coverage

tasks.title: Create admin API: subscriber list and stats endpoints
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/v1/admin/newsletter/subscribers uses createAdminListRoute with NewsletterSubscriberAdminSearchSchema
Supports page, pageSize, status, locale, source, search (email safeIlike) filters
GET /api/v1/admin/newsletter/subscribers/stats returns { totalActive, totalPending, totalUnsubscribed, totalBounced, totalComplained }
Both require NEWSLETTER_SUBSCRIBER_VIEW permission
Integration tests cover pagination, status filter, locale filter, stats endpoint
tasks.description: Create apps/api/src/routes/newsletter/admin/index.ts with subscriber list and stats endpoints. Use createAdminListRoute factory for the list. Filters use safeIlike() for email search per project convention (never raw ilike()). Both endpoints require NEWSLETTER_SUBSCRIBER_VIEW permission. Write integration tests.

qualityGate.tests: integration tests for filters and stats

tasks.title: Create admin API: campaign CRUD endpoints (list, create, get, update, delete)
acceptanceCriteria: GET /admin/newsletter/campaigns: paginated list with status filter and search
POST /admin/newsletter/campaigns: creates draft campaign, returns 201
GET /admin/newsletter/campaigns/:id: returns single campaign
PATCH /admin/newsletter/campaigns/:id: updates draft campaign, returns 409 if not draft
DELETE /admin/newsletter/campaigns/:id: soft deletes, returns 409 if sending/sent
VIEW endpoints require NEWSLETTER_CAMPAIGN_VIEW, write endpoints require NEWSLETTER_CAMPAIGN_WRITE
Integration tests cover CRUD happy paths and 409 error cases
tasks.description: Extend the admin newsletter router with campaign CRUD endpoints. PATCH returns 409 campaign_not_editable if status is not draft. DELETE returns 409 campaign_not_deletable if status is sending or sent. Use existing route factory patterns. Write integration tests for all endpoints including error cases.

tasks.title: Create admin API: test-send, send, cancel, metrics, errors endpoints
acceptanceCriteria: POST /campaigns/:id/test-send: sends to admin's own email, returns { sent: true, sentTo }
POST /campaigns/:id/send: returns 202 { dispatched: true, enqueued, softcapped } or 200 { dispatched: false, reason: 'no_eligible_subscribers' } or 409 campaign_not_sendable
Rate limit on /send: 1 req/min per admin
POST /campaigns/:id/cancel: returns 200 { cancelled: true, skipped } or 409 campaign_not_cancellable
GET /campaigns/:id/metrics: returns full metrics object (no-cache header)
GET /campaigns/:id/errors: paginated failed deliveries with masked email
All action endpoints require NEWSLETTER_CAMPAIGN_SEND; view endpoints require NEWSLETTER_CAMPAIGN_VIEW
Integration tests for send (202, 200 no-eligible, 409), cancel (200, 409)
tasks.description: Add action and metrics endpoints to the admin newsletter router. test-send sends to the admin's own email with '[PRUEBA] ' prefix. send endpoint calls NewsletterCampaignService.send(), returns 202 with enqueued count. cancel calls NewsletterCampaignService.cancel(). metrics endpoint returns live data (no-cache). errors endpoint returns masked emails. Rate limit send to 1/min per admin.

qualityGate.tests: integration tests for all action endpoints

tasks.title: Install TipTap extensions and create RichTextEditor component in apps/admin
acceptanceCriteria: @tiptap/extension-image, @tiptap/extension-link, @tiptap/extension-underline, @tiptap/extension-text-align installed
Confirm @tiptap/react and @tiptap/starter-kit are already present (don't duplicate)
RichTextEditor component: accepts value (TipTap JSON), onChange callback, disabled prop
Toolbar: Bold, Italic, Underline, H2, H3, Bullet list, Ordered list, Blockquote, Link, Divider, Clear formatting
Keyboard-operable per accessibility requirements
getHTML() output uses renderTiptapContent() from @repo/utils for preview

tasks.title: Implement Brevo webhook endpoint (POST /public/webhooks/brevo)

tasks.description: Create apps/api/src/routes/webhooks/brevo.ts with signature verification using timingSafeEqual (from Node.js crypto, NOT a naive string comparison). tasks.title: Add bullmq dependency and create BullMQ newsletter dispatch worker
acceptanceCriteria: bullmq ^5.x added to apps/api/package.json
startNewsletterWorker({ redis, db, brevoClient, logger }) exported from worker file
Worker uses queue name 'hospeda-newsletter-dispatch'
Worker config: concurrency=HOSPEDA_NEWSLETTER_WORKER_CONCURRENCY (default 5), attempts=3, backoff exponential 30s
removeOnComplete: { age: 3600 } and removeOnFail: { age: 86400 } configured
Worker calls NewsletterDeliveryService.processBatch() with job data
Structured logging per tech-analysis §10.1 (newsletter.batch.attempt/success/failure)
Sentry transaction wrapping processBatch()
pnpm typecheck passes in apps/api
tasks.description: Add bullmq dependency. Create the BullMQ worker module per tech-analysis §8.2. Worker processes jobs from the hospeda-newsletter-dispatch queue by calling NewsletterDeliveryService.processBatch(). Job memory cleanup configured via removeOnComplete/removeOnFail. Structured logging and Sentry tracing applied.

qualityGate.tests: unit test: worker processes a mock job and calls processBatch()

tasks.title: Integrate worker startup and graceful shutdown into apps/api/src/index.ts
acceptanceCriteria: startNewsletterWorker called after serve() resolves and after startCronScheduler(), gated by NODE_ENV !== 'test'
Graceful shutdown order updated: stopCronJobs() → await worker.close() → closeSentry → destroyUserPermissionsCache → disconnectRedis → closeDatabase → server.close()
worker.close() awaited (max 30s drain) before Redis disconnect
No worker startup in test environment
pnpm typecheck passes in apps/api
tasks.description: Wire startNewsletterWorker into the API startup sequence per tech-analysis §8.2. Update gracefulShutdown() to include worker.close() before Redis disconnect. This order prevents partial batch sends on rolling deploys — BullMQ drains in-flight jobs before the Redis connection closes.

qualityGate.tests: n/a — startup/shutdown covered by smoke tests

tasks.title: Write ADR for newsletter architecture (BullMQ + Brevo batch + embedded worker)
acceptanceCriteria: ADR documents: decision to use BullMQ over QStash, reason for embedded worker vs separate service, Brevo batch API choice, HMAC token design
Records alternatives considered (QStash, dedicated worker process, Resend)
Documents tradeoffs and why BullMQ on existing Redis was chosen
ADR number does not conflict with existing ADRs (verify docs/decisions/ for next available number)
tasks.description: Write an Architecture Decision Record documenting the dispatch engine choices. Key decisions: BullMQ over QStash (no new infra, same Redis), embedded worker vs separate process (simpler ops for MVP scale), Brevo batch API (single transport, no extra deps), HMAC token approach. Check existing ADR numbers before choosing ADR-021.
